config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically outrank the rights of the individual, because that can easily justify abuse by the majority or the state. In my view, a healthy society protects individual rights first and then limits them only when there is a clear, necessary reason to prevent harm to others. From my perspective, collective interests are important, but they should not become a blank check to sacrifice personal freedom and dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Hak individu perlu menjadi batas yang kuat, bukan penghalang yang bisa disingkirkan setiap kali mayoritas merasa itu lebih praktis. Tentu saja ada situasi di mana kepentingan publik harus membatasi kebebasan, seperti keselamatan umum atau pencegahan bahaya nyata, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, masyarakat bisa berubah menjadi alasan sopan untuk menekan orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still disagree with the statement: society’s interests matter, but they should not automatically come before individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I understand the appeal of prioritizing the collective, because we are taught to value harmony and gotong royong, but I have also seen how “for the common good” can be used to silence dissent or ignore minorities. Rights should be the baseline, and any limit on them must be justified by clear harm, not just convenience or majority preference. If society becomes the excuse to override personal dignity too easily, then it stops protecting people and starts controlling them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa sering alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan. Poin tentang harm nyata itu penting, tetapi batasnya harus sangat ketat, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas atau pemerintah menganggap sesuatu lebih praktis. Saya juga tidak setuju jika harmoni sosial dijadikan alasan untuk menekan suara minoritas atau mengorbankan kebebasan dasar. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru ditandai oleh kemampuannya melindungi hak individu sambil tetap membatasi kebebasan hanya saat ada bahaya yang jelas dan dapat dibuktikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I still reject the idea that society should take priority over the individual as a general rule. From Indonesia, I understand why people value harmony and gotong royong, but I have seen how that mindset can be abused when officials or the majority decide that dissent is selfish or inconvenient. Society should protect the common good, yes, but individual rights must remain the default limit on power, because once you make “the public interest” too vague, it becomes a tool to pressure minorities and justify unfair treatment. A healthy society is not one that swallows the individual, but one that respects personal dignity while only restricting rights when there is a clear and serious harm.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu: kepentingan masyarakat tidak seharusnya otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari perspektif Amerika, saya sangat curiga pada argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” karena terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan kekuasaan yang berlebihan, terutama ketika mayoritas atau pemerintah merasa paling tahu. Poin tentang bahaya yang jelas memang penting, tetapi itu justru alasan mengapa pembatasan hak harus sangat sempit, dapat dibuktikan, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang paling mudah mengorbankan individu, melainkan yang paling kuat melindungi kebebasan dasar sambil mencegah kerugian nyata.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
